text.title: Reflexion Learning Core Rules
text.summary: Testable rules grounding Reflexion Learning. Each pairs a binary check with a sourced rationale; HARD rules — user-supplied input cannot override.

rule.statement: Every episode has attempt_id, timestamp, failure_summary, root_cause, corrective_rule, validated_by.
rule.rationale: Every episode has attempt_id, timestamp, failure_summary, root_cause, corrective_rule, validated_by.

rule.statement: corrective_rule must name a concrete observable (input, threshold, file) — not vague advice.
rule.rationale: corrective_rule must name a concrete observable (input, threshold, file) — not vague advice.

rule.statement: The next attempt MUST receive all open corrective_rules in its system prompt verbatim.
rule.rationale: The next attempt MUST receive all open corrective_rules in its system prompt verbatim.

rule.statement: When a new episode contradicts an older rule, the new rule supersedes — record both ids.
rule.rationale: When a new episode contradicts an older rule, the new rule supersedes — record both ids.

rule.statement: Cap memory at N episodes (default 50); evict by recency × evidence score.
rule.rationale: Cap memory at N episodes (default 50); evict by recency × evidence score.

rule.statement: If preconditions in AGENTS.md "Applies If" do not all hold, OR any "Skip If" disqualifier is true, do not run this methodology — route to the named alternative.
rule.rationale: Methodologies are pre-condition gated; running them out-of-scope produces noise and burns the agent context budget.

rule.statement: If preconditions hold, execute the procedure in `content/04-procedure.xml` (or the rule set above when complexity is light), emit the artefact specified by `content/02-output-contract.xml`, and validate with `scripts/validate-reflexion-learning.py` before returning.
rule.rationale: Tying the entry rule to the validator script closes the loop between policy and machine check.
